reasoning:
The baseline price will be established on 2026-02-01 (a Sunday), which falls back to the Friday, January 30 market close price of $24.61. Over the past three months, HRL has exhibited a mild positive linear trend (roughly +0.03 per day) and surged 7% in early December following an optimistic 2026 outlook and a strong Q4 earnings release. The ex-dividend date occurred earlier in January, meaning there will not be a dividend-related stock drop (price reduction) between February 1 and March 3.

For the February 8 resolution (one week horizon), the stock only needs to be above $24.61. With the current positive drift and the lack of short-term negative catalysts, the probability slightly favors an increase, calculating to roughly P=0.57 (extrapolating the 0.03 daily drift with measured recent volatility).

For the March 3 resolution (one month horizon), HRL will likely release its Q1 2026 earnings in late February (projected around Feb 26, based on past schedules). Earnings reports introduce substantial volatility (bidirectional jumps). This substantially increases uncertainty regarding the March 3 close price, widening the distribution relative to the underlying drift. Conforming to the principle that uncertainty increases over longer forecast horizons and major catalyst events shrink the relative impact of underlying drift, the probability for March 3 shrinks closer to a coin toss at 0.54.
